title: Introduction to Programming – South Pole Quiz

explanation.`A: 2`, `B: 1`: Correct. Breaking at y == 2 prints icebergs 0 through 2 for each seal, and returning at x == 1 stops the program after the second seal (x = 0 and x = 1).
explanation.`A: 1`, `B: 2`: Incorrect. Breaking at y == 1 would stop printing at iceberg 1 per seal, and waiting for x == 2 would incorrectly print a third seal.
explanation.`A: 2 - x`, `B: 1`: Incorrect. Making the inner break condition dependent on x causes Seal 1 to stop moving prematurely at iceberg 1 instead of iceberg 2.
explanation.`A: 2`, `B: x + y == 3`: Incorrect. The variable y is out of scope in the outer loop, so this code would fail to compile.

explanation.`List<String> souvenirBottle = new Array: Correct. ArrayList preserves insertion order and supports the get(int index) method needed to access elements at specific positions.
explanation.`Stack<String> souvenirBottle = new Stac: Incorrect. Stack is designed for LIFO operations (push/pop) and is not intended for random index-based access.
explanation.`Set<Integer> souvenirBottle = new HashS: Incorrect. Set collections are unordered and do not support the get() method for accessing elements by index.
explanation.`Queue souvenirTube = new Queue();`: Incorrect. Queue is an interface designed for FIFO processing and does not support random access via an index. It also cannot be instantiated directly.

explanation.`System.out.print(mySeal.swim() + \" \": Correct. Casting to SouthPoleSeal grants access to information(), which calls super.title() and returns "Generic-Mammal". Polymorphism ensures mySeal.swim() dispatches to the overridden version, returning "Dives".
Incorrect. Due to polymorphism, mySeal.title() executes the overridden version in SouthPoleSeal and returns "Brave-Swimmer" instead of "Generic-Mammal".
explanation.`System.out.print(mySeal.title() + \" \": Incorrect. This reverses the output order and also incorrectly calls the overridden title(), returning "Brave-Swimmer" rather than "Generic-Mammal".
Incorrect. The super keyword is instance-specific and cannot be used inside a static main method to access parent class members.

learning_objectives: Apply nested loops with break and return to control program flow and predict the resulting output
Select the correct Java collection (ArrayList) based on index-based access requirements using get()
Predict method dispatch under polymorphism and use super and downcasting to access parent-class behaviour
